title: arceuthos drupacea
intro: Arceuthos drupacea, also known as the Chinese rowan or mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub native to China, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to promote digestion and reduce fever. The health benefits of Arceuthos drupacea include its potential to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, improve immune function, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Arceuthos drupacea is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried berry, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulations.
